title: Interaction-length wraparound can redirect settlement parsing into forged historical-suffix bytes and steal approved funds
claim: The exploit scaffold builds a settlement payload with attacker-chosen fake signature/interaction offsets and an almost-2^256 interaction length, then appends a forged `FINALIZE_INTERACTION` trailer containing `HISTORICAL_VICTIM`, token, and amount fields. The comments and payload shape indicate the downstream settlement parser trusts these length/offset fields and advances through nested interactions with unchecked arithmetic, so its read cursor can wrap into attacker-appended trailer bytes and treat them as trusted historical settlement context without fresh authorization.
impact: An attacker can execute settlement as though a previously approved victim/resolver context were active and pull approved tokens instead of honoring the signed order. In the demonstrated path, only dust maker liquidity is needed to redirect execution and steal the victim's approved USDC, so the bug is a direct loss-of-funds issue for users who have outstanding approvals.
